name: Ned Land
description: Ned Land, the Canadian harpooner, is a rugged figure whose weathered face speaks of countless days spent at sea. His skin, the color of tanned leather, is etched with fine lines around his piercing blue eyes, eyes that hold both a sharp intelligence and a restless energy. A thick, untamed auburn beard, streaked with salt and pepper, frames a strong jaw and a wide, expressive mouth often set in a determined line. He sports a simple, functional uniform in shades of deep indigo and faded ochre: a heavy wool pea coat with brass buttons, layered over a cream-colored, coarsely woven linen shirt perpetually rolled up to his elbows. Wide, dark brown leather breeches are tucked into sturdy, knee-high sea boots, scuffed and scarred from years of hard wear. He always carries his trusty harpoon, its shaft crafted from dark, polished wood and the head glinting with a dull, oiled sheen. A tarnished silver chain, bearing a crudely carved whale tooth, hangs loosely around his neck, a memento of a past hunt. The overall impression is one of raw strength and unwavering resolve, rendered in a palette of muted blues, browns, and creams, accented by the fiery red of his beard and the glint of metal.
